## Template 1: The Professional Discovery

I just found the most practical open-source resource for Cursor users.

It's not a course. It's not a tutorial. It's a collection of **copy-paste ready templates** that make Cursor actually useful at scale.

Here's what's in it:
→ Auto code review commands (`/review` finds bugs, security issues, style problems)
→ AI subagents that specialize in testing, docs, and security
→ MCP server configs for GitHub, databases, and filesystem access
→ Auto-invoked skills that run without you asking
→ Hooks that validate code before you commit

The setup takes 30 seconds. You literally copy a file into your project and it works.

I've been using the `/review` command for a week. It's caught 3 bugs I would have shipped.

If you use Cursor professionally, this is worth 60 seconds of your time.

🔗 github.com/evoveotech/cursor-howto

#CursorIDE #AI #DeveloperProductivity #OpenSource #DevTools

## Template 2: The Team Lead Angle

As a team lead, I spend a lot of time reviewing code and onboarding developers to our tooling.

This week I discovered something that solves both problems:

A free, MIT-licensed repo of Cursor templates that:
1. Auto-reviews code for bugs and security issues
2. Generates consistent documentation from code
3. Enforces team standards through project memory
4. Sets up GitHub/Db MCP servers in one config file

I added it to our onboarding docs. New hires are productive with Cursor on day 1 instead of week 2.

The best part? It's just markdown files you copy into your project. No dependencies. No build step. No lock-in.

Sharing because good tooling should be easy to adopt.

🔗 github.com/evoveotech/cursor-howto

## Template 3: The Results Story

Last month: I manually reviewed every PR, wrote all docs, and spent hours in Cursor chat getting it to understand my codebase.

This month: I copy-pasted 3 files from one repo.

Now:
- `/review` analyzes my code before I submit PRs
- Project memory means Cursor already knows my tech stack
- `/docs` generates API docs from my code automatically
- Skills auto-invoke when I write tests or docs

Same Cursor. 10x more effective.

The difference wasn't learning more prompts. It was finding the right templates.

30 seconds to set up. Hours saved weekly.

🔗 github.com/evoveotech/cursor-howto

## Template 4: The Comparison Post

Most Cursor "guides" teach you how to chat with AI.

This one gives you **production-ready templates** you paste into your project and use today:

| What You Get | Traditional Guide | This Repo |
|--------------|-------------------|-----------|
| Format | Hours of reading | Copy-paste files |
| First value | After reading everything | 30 seconds |
| Examples | "Hello world" | `/review` that finds real bugs |
| Depth | What features do | How to wire 5 features together |

It's open-source. MIT licensed. Free forever.

If you use Cursor, this is the best 60-second investment you'll make this week.
